title: EVEROLIMUS
description: Everolimus is a semi-synthetic derivative of rapamycin that acts as a potent inhibitor of the mechanistic target of rapamycin (mTOR) complex 1. It is primarily used clinically for its immunosuppressant and anti-proliferative properties, though it is increasingly studied for its potential to modulate biological aging and enhance healthspan.

research.title: Long-Term Use of Everolimus in Patients with Tuberous Sclerosis Complex: Final Results from the EXIST-1 Study
research.summary: This study demonstrated that long-term everolimus use leads to sustained reductions in tumor volume and safely manages multi-system manifestations over several years.

sentiment.summary: Users generally view everolimus as a more bioavailable or manageable alternative to rapamycin for longevity, reporting high satisfaction with its dosing profile despite standard mTOR inhibitor side effects.

effects.label: Sustained Efficacy:
effects.description: Long-term clinical data shows that beneficial effects on tumor reduction and lesion control are maintained over 4+ years.

effects.label: Bioavailability:
effects.description: Evidence suggests everolimus has better oral bioavailability and a shorter half-life than sirolimus, allowing for more precise dosing.

effects.label: Geroprotection:
effects.description: Research indicates it is a leading pharmacological agent for extending maximal lifespan and improving healthspan in mammalian models.

dosage.label: Clinical Starting Dose:
dosage.description: 4.5 mg/m2/day titrated to achieve a target blood trough of 5–15 ng/mL for medical conditions.

dosage.label: Longevity Protocol:
dosage.description: Users often transition to intermittent weekly dosing (e.g., 5-10mg once weekly) to minimize chronic immune suppression.

dosage.label: Individualized Monitoring:
dosage.description: Bioavailability varies significantly between individuals, making routine blood level testing essential for safety.

dosage.label: Formulation Differences:
dosage.description: Commercial tablets have significantly higher bioavailability (roughly 3x) compared to some compounded versions.

sideEffects.label: Stomatitis:
sideEffects.description: Inflammatory sores in the mouth are the most frequently reported side effect, occurring in over 40% of patients.

sideEffects.label: Mouth Ulceration:
sideEffects.description: Small, painful ulcers that typically decrease in frequency as the body adapts to the compound.

sideEffects.label: Dyslipidemia:
sideEffects.description: Potential for increased blood lipid and glucose levels, requiring periodic metabolic panels.

sideEffects.label: Dermatological Issues:
sideEffects.description: While it can clear lesions, some users experience acne-like rashes or thin skin.

availability.label: Prescription Requirement:
availability.description: Everolimus is an FDA-approved medication requiring a prescription, usually for TSC or oncology.

availability.label: Longevity Access:
availability.description: Often sourced off-label through specialized longevity clinics or via international pharmacies for anti-aging purposes.
